_purpose: Engine-lookupable drafting conventions consumed by SLD, db-layout, lighting-layout, riser, schematic, small-power, cable-containment skills

BS1192.title: BS 1192:2007+A2:2016 — Collaborative production of architectural, engineering and construction information
BS1192.scope: UK information management + layer naming + file naming + revision conventions

ISO19650.title: ISO 19650:2018 (Parts 1-5) — Organization and digitization of information about buildings
ISO19650.scope: International CDE workflows, federated information management, security

ISO5457.title: ISO 5457:1999/A1:2010 — Technical product documentation — Sizes and layout of drawing sheets
ISO5457.scope: Sheet sizes A0-A4 + drawing area + title block region

ISO7200.title: ISO 7200:2004 — Technical product documentation — Data fields in title blocks and document headers

ISO128.title: ISO 128 (multiple parts) — Technical drawings — General principles of presentation

ISO129.title: ISO 129-1:2018 — Technical product documentation — Presentation of dimensions and tolerances

ISO9431.title: ISO 9431:1990 — Construction drawings — Spaces for drawing and for text, and title blocks on drawing sheets

BS5536.title: BS 5536:1978 — Recommendations for preparation of technical drawings and diagrams for acceptance purposes
BS5536.scope: UK reading of ISO 128 line conventions

_note: Skills query this index file as the discovery entry point. Per-standard data lives in the named folder. Jurisdiction-to-primary-standards mapping tells the runtime which standards to consult for a given example's jurisdiction.
